description_long: This is a rounded, earthenware vase with a bulbous body and a short, wide neck. The surface is decorated with white geometric and cross motifs, arranged symmetrically along the body, contrasting with the reddish-brown clay base. Two small, looped handles are attached to the neck, and a third, more pronounced handle extends from the neck to the shoulder of the vase. The finish appears matte and slightly rough, with visible signs of age and wear, suggesting it may be handmade or inspired by traditional pottery. The opening at the top is circular and wide enough for small bouquets or as a decorative vessel. The overall appearance is rustic and artisanal, making it suitable for display in urban parks, plazas, or as a street-side decorative element.
description: A rustic, rounded earthenware vase with white geometric motifs and three handles, featuring a matte, weathered finish.
description_view_0: This view highlights the prominent single handle extending from the neck to the shoulder, with geometric patterns visible on the front.
description_view_1: The two looped side handles are clearly visible, and the symmetrical cross motifs are centered on the body.
description_view_2: The single handle is seen from the opposite side, with the geometric patterns continuing around the body.
description_view_3: Both looped handles are visible, and the cross motifs are more pronounced, showing the full decorative pattern.
